title: Philip K. Dick: Contemporary Critical Interpretations
author: Samuel J. Umland (ed.)
card_summary: An edited collection of critical essays on Philip K. Dick published by Greenwood Press, a major academic publisher. At 120 pages, the volume brings together contemporary scholarly interpretations of Dick's work. Samuel J. Umland is a scholar who has contributed significantly to the academic study of Dick's fiction.
page_summary:
Philip K. Dick: Contemporary Critical Interpretations is an edited collection of scholarly essays compiled by Samuel J. Umland and published by Greenwood Press, a major academic publisher. At 120 pages, the volume collects contemporary scholarly readings of Dick's fiction from multiple critical perspectives.

The PDF is a scanned document at 31.76 MB with no OCR text layer, indicating it was digitized from a physical copy. Greenwood Press (later absorbed into ABC-CLIO) was a prominent publisher of academic reference works and critical studies, and their publication of this volume reflects the institutional recognition of Dick as a significant literary figure worthy of sustained academic attention.

Umland's collection represents the state of Dick scholarship in the mid-1990s, a period when academic interest in Dick was accelerating rapidly. The volume's publication by an established academic press helped legitimize Dick studies within the broader field of literary criticism and provided a platform for diverse critical approaches to his work.
